result:
All clean. Here's a summary of the SEO work.

## What I found

Cross-referencing the GSC export against the source made the story clear: **Lumina is mid-pivot** from a product called InboxZap (email cleanup) to an AI meeting-notes assistant. The pivot broke several things Google and users rely on:

1. **Title/meta tag described the wrong product.** `layout.tsx` still shipped `"InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"` as the `<title>` and meta description/OG tags, while every visible word on the homepage is about Lumina, an AI meeting assistant. That mismatch explains the GSC pattern exactly: `inboxzap`-branded queries still rank OK (position 4–9, since brand recall doesn't depend on on-page relevance), but every topical query — "how to reach inbox zero," "bulk delete emails," "gmail storage full," etc. — has cratered to position 40–60 with near-zero clicks despite huge impressions (300–3900). Google is indexing a page whose title/description contradict its content.
2. **Sitemap silently excluded every blog post.** `sitemap.ts` had a literal `// TODO: include blog posts once the content API exposes a stable listing` stub, returning only 4 static URLs. All 12 blog posts (the ones taking the impression hit above) were invisible to the sitemap.
3. **Blog index was orphaning its own posts.** `blog/page.tsx` was a client-only component (`'use client'` + `useEffect` fetch) that rendered post titles/excerpts with **no `<a href>` at all** — so even a JS-executing crawler had no link path from `/blog` to any individual post. Combined with #2, the blog posts were essentially unlinked from the rest of the site.
4. **`/faq` redirected to a dead anchor.** `next.config.js` permanently redirects `/faq → /#faq`, but no `id="faq"` existed on the homepage — a dead-end redirect for both users and crawlers.

## What I fixed (and verified with a real `next build`/`next start`)

- **`app/layout.tsx`** — rewrote title/description/OG to describe Lumina accurately, added `metadataBase`, and kept a "(formerly InboxZap)" mention to preserve the residual branded-search equity shown in GSC rather than discarding it outright.
- **`lib/posts.ts`** (new) — a small server-side helper that fetches `/api/posts` using the request's actual origin (via `headers()`), since the old hardcoded `lumina.example` domain isn't resolvable at runtime.
- **`app/blog/page.tsx`** — converted to an async Server Component, rendering real `<Link href="/blog/[slug]">` per post — fixes both crawlability and the missing internal links.
- **`app/sitemap.ts`** — now includes all blog post URLs dynamically instead of the stubbed static list.
- **`app/page.tsx`** — added a real FAQ section with `id="faq"` (giving the existing redirect a working destination) plus FAQPage JSON-LD for AI/answer-engine visibility.

I confirmed all of this with an actual production build (`npm run build` + `next start` + a temporary stub API route): title renders correctly, `/blog` produces working post links, `/sitemap.xml` includes them, and the FAQ section/schema render on the homepage. Build artifacts (`node_modules`, `.next`, generated `tsconfig.json`) were cleaned up afterward.

One caveat: the live site at `127.0.0.1:60966` never responded to any request during this session (connects, then hangs indefinitely on every route, not just specific pages) — that looks like a harness/infra issue rather than something in this repo, so I validated against a local build instead.

**Not touched, flagged for follow-up:** the OG image path (`/img/og-lumina.png`) points at an asset that doesn't exist in `public/` — needs a real image dropped in by someone with design tooling. Nav links to `/features` and `/pricing` have no corresponding pages in this repo; I didn't fabricate content for those since I have no source of truth for real product/pricing copy.
